name: Bradshaw v. Richey
question:
1. Under Ohio law, can the doctrine of transferred intent be applied to aggravated felony murder?

2. Should a federal appellate court rely on evidence that was not properly presented to the state courts in determining whether a defendant received ineffective assistance of counsel?
description: A case in which the Court held the doctrine of transferred intent applied to aggravated felony murder in the state of Ohio and that a federal appellate court should not rely on evidence that was not properly presented to the state courts in deciding an ineffective assistance of counsel claim.
